_README: Standardized replay format produced by make_replay_dataset.py and consumed by replay_bench.py. ONE such object per line in a *.replay.jsonl file (this file is pretty-printed with truncated content for readability). The replayer slices messages[:prefix_len] per turn — see field docs below.

_field_docs.session_id: stable id for this conversation (one agentic session = one trajectory)
_field_docs.entry_point: provenance: the KernelBook task class name
_field_docs.kernelbook_uuid: provenance: KernelBook row id (null if unknown)
_field_docs.messages: the FULL reconstructed conversation, flat OpenAI chat format, stored ONCE. system_prompt.md is message 0 (shared prefix); then user(task)/assistant/user(tool result)/... alternation
_field_docs.turns: one entry per assistant turn to replay (this is the unit of load)
_field_docs.turns[].prefix_len: request body = messages[:prefix_len]; i.e. everything BEFORE this assistant turn. Growing per turn -> long shared prefix -> KV prefix-cache reuse
_field_docs.turns[].max_tokens: tokens to generate (with ignore_eos) = the recorded decode load for this turn
_field_docs.turns[].delay_before_s: recorded inter-turn idle/think gap (capped). ONLY used if replay_bench --use-think-time; ignored by default
_field_docs.turns[].rec_input_tokens: ANALYSIS ONLY: Claude's recorded prompt tokens for this turn (not sent to server)
_field_docs.turns[].rec_output_tokens: ANALYSIS ONLY: recorded output tokens for this turn (== max_tokens)
_field_docs.turns[].rec_cache_read_tokens: ANALYSIS ONLY: Claude's recorded cache-read tokens for this turn
_field_docs.recorded: ANALYSIS ONLY: session-level ground truth (num_turns, correct, final speedup)
